easy.question: Which of the following is a common advertising objective?
What is the term used to describe the process of delivering an advertising message to a target audience?
Which of the following is an example of a traditional advertising medium?
What is the purpose of a call-to-action in advertising?

options.a: To provide contact information for the advertiser
options.b: To highlight the unique selling proposition
options.c: To encourage immediate response or action

easy.question: What is the term used to describe the process of selecting specific media channels to reach the target audience?
Which of the following is an example of a demographic segmentation variable?
What is the primary purpose of a headline in an advertisement?

options.a: To introduce the brand or product
options.c: To grab the attention of the audience
options.d: To list the features and benefits

easy.question: Which of the following is a key element of effective advertising copy?
What is the term used to describe the process of evaluating the effectiveness of an advertising campaign?
Which of the following is an example of a digital advertising format?

average.question: The target audience for an advertising campaign should be clearly defined.
Advertising is only about selling products or services.
Influencer marketing involves partnering with influential individuals to promote products or services.
The unique selling proposition (USP) refers to the distinctive features of a product or service.
Media planning involves determining the best time and place to deliver advertising messages.
Brand positioning is the process of creating a unique and favorable perception of a brand in the minds of consumers.
AIDA stands for Attention, Interest, Desire, and Action, which represents the stages of the consumer decision-making process.
Frequency refers to the number of times an advertisement is shown to a target audience.
Conversion rate measures the percentage of people who take a desired action after seeing an advertisement.
Programmatic advertising involves buying and selling advertising space through an automated system.

difficult.question: __________ is the process of identifying the target audience and understanding their characteristics and preferences.
__________ is a marketing strategy in which two or more brands collaborate on a promotional campaign.
__________ is a pricing model in which advertisers pay only when a specific action, such as a click or purchase, occurs.
__________ is a marketing technique that involves creating content that is useful or entertaining to the target audience.
The __________ rate measures the percentage of people who open an email or click on a link within an email.
__________ refers to the percentage of people who see an advertisement and then visit the advertiser's website within a specific time frame.
__________ is a method of advertising in which the ad is seamlessly integrated into the content of a website or social media platform.
__________ refers to the process of identifying and reaching out to individuals who are likely to be interested in a product or service.
The __________ model of advertising involves advertisers bidding for ad space in real-time auctions.
__________ is the process of monitoring and responding to customer feedback and inquiries on social media platforms.
